title: Executive Order 10175—Amending Executive Order No. 7908 of June 9, 1938, and Executive Order No. 10046 of March 24, 1949

flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment updating agency-name references and land-transfer conditions under existing statutory authority.
A clear, specific amendment made upon the joint recommendation of the Secretaries of Agriculture and the Interior.
Two related amendments to two related prior orders, both coherently serving the same land-management program.
A land-management amendment creating no compliance regime against private parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing land-management framework amendment, not a temporary-authority grant.
The added proviso ensures lands acquired in exchange remain subject to the same statutory conditions as the original lands — a protective anti-loophole provision, not a removal of oversight.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, technical amendment to an established land-management program.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Notable for a protective feature: the added proviso closes a potential loophole by ensuring lands acquired through exchange remain subject to the same program conditions as the original lands.
